items.title: Former Chelsea and Leeds owner Bates dies aged 94
items.summary: Former Chelsea and Leeds United owner Ken Bates has died aged 94, Chelsea announced on Saturday.

items.title: From Arsenal jeers to Swiss tears: Xhaka's redemption
items.summary: Granit Xhaka has battled back from being booed by his own fans at Arsenal to being one of the most influential players for club and country as he looks to help Switzerland make history at the World Cup.

items.title: Klopp agrees deal in principle to manage Germany
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp is on the verge of becoming Germany's new head coach after reaching an agreement in principle with the German Football Association [DFB], according to reports in Germany.

items.title: Much-changed Ireland beat Japan in Nations Championship as debutants impress
items.summary: A much-changed Ireland side overcame Japan 36-20 to make it two wins from two in the Nations Championship.

items.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
items.summary: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

items.summary: SOUTH AFRICA star Jayden Adams has died just weeks after playing at the World Cup. The Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder passed away aged 25. items.title: Chelsea want permanent Garnacho sale this summer
items.summary: Chelsea are prepared to sell Alejandro Garnacho for £50m this summer, preferring a permanent departure over a loan proposal.

items.title: Garnacho absent from Chelsea pre-season training
items.summary: Alejandro Garnacho has not yet joined up with Chelsea for pre-season as the club actively look to sell the winger.

items.title: Ken Bates dead aged 94: Tributes pour in for ex-Chelsea owner who bought Blues for £1 and saved club from financial ruin
items.summary: FORMER Chelsea chairman Ken Bates has died at 94. The London-born businessman bought the Blues for a single pound in 1982, and went on to act as owner and chairman until 2003. items.title: Kane says Haaland 'completely different' than me
